channel.title: 2026-05-05 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-05-05 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: Force-free molecular dynamics through autoregressive equivariant networks
item.description:
📚 Nature Machine Intelligence
👤 Fabian L. Thiemann, Thiago Reschützegger, Massimiliano Esposito, Tseden Taddese, Juan D. Olarte-Plata, Fausto Martelli
Nature Machine Intelligence, Published online: 05 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s42256-026-01227-7 Thiemann et al. introduce TrajCast, a neural network that bypasses force calculations to directly predict atomic trajectories, enabling time steps up to 30 times longer while accurately reproducing physical properties of molecules and materials.

item.title: SR-CGCNN: Shared Recurrent Convolution in Crystal Graph Neural Networks for Materials Property Prediction
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Satadeep Bhattacharjee
arXiv:2605.01304v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Crystal graph neural networks predict materials properties by propagating information through local atomic environments. In conventional crystal graph convolutional neural networks (CGCNNs), this propagation depth is increased by stacking independently parameterized convolutional layers. This coupling between message-passing depth and parameter count raises a simple question: can repeated application of the same learned local update recover most of the benefit of a deeper CGCNN? We address this question by introducing a shared-recurrent CGCNN (SR-CGCNN), in which the main crystal-graph convolutional weights are tied across recurrent message-passing steps. The graph construction, pooling operation, and prediction head are kept unchanged, allo…

item.title: 磁性材料的全从头算自旋-晶格动力学框架
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Xianxi Zhang, Hongyu Yu, Liangliang Hong, Hongjun Xiang
该工作提出了一套完全从头算的自旋-晶格动力学（SLD）方法，并集成于VASP软件中，可在每个时间步通过自洽约束矩密度泛函计算获得原子间力和有效磁场。该方法在涵盖铁磁、非共线及几何阻挫有序的四种材料上得到验证，均能从随机初始条件恢复正确的磁基态。SLD轨迹还为磁性机器学习势函数提供物理关联的训练数据，以BiFeO₃为例，相比随机自旋构型训练可将能量平均绝对误差降低约一个数量级。该框架为磁性材料有限温度自旋-晶格耦合现象提供了实用的第一性原理途径。

item.title: Hybrid EKF - Machine Learning Framework for SOC -SOH Estimation in Sodium ION Batteries
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 KANDREGULA PRIYASWI, K RAMA SUDHA, A SUPRAJA
Reliable battery management in emerging sodium-ion systems depends heavily on how well internal states can be estimated, especially parameters such as state of charge (SOC) and state of health(SOH). While sodium -ion chemistry offers clear advantage’s in terms of material abundance and cost, its electrochemical behaviour introduces Unique’s challenges for conventional estimation techniques, including reduced voltage sensitivity over extended SOC regions and pronounced temperature-dependent resistance variations. In this work, a unified estimation strategy is developed by incorporating a physics -driven Extended Kalman Filter(EKF) for improved performance with a lightweight neural network designed to compensate for model inaccuracies .In this work, the EKF is applied using a three-RC equiv…

item.title: Physics-informed neural networks for form-finding of unilateral membrane structures
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Luigi Sibille, Sigrid Adriaenssens, Carlo Olivieri
arXiv:2605.00863v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Form-finding of unilateral membrane structures is commonly addressed by solving equilibrium equations with Finite Element Methods (FEMs). This paper investigates Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) as an alternative, where the equilibrium equation is enforced by minimizing its residual at collocation points during neural-network training rather than by solving a mesh-based discretized system. This approach is well suited to form-finding problems based on Membrane Equilibrium Analysis (MEA), in which the unknown membrane surface is governed by a second-order elliptic Partial Differential Equation (PDE) with Dirichlet boundary conditions. Two PINN formulations are proposed and compared: a soft-Boundary Condition (soft-BC) approach, wher…

item.title: 2026 Roadmap on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning for Smart Manufacturing
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jay Lee, Hanqi Su, Marco Macchi, Adalberto Polenghi, Wei Wu, Zhiheng Zhao, George Q. Huang, Kiva Allgood, Devendra Jain, Benedikt Gieger, Vibhor Pandhare, Soumyabrata Bhattacharjee, Ram Mohril, Lingbao Kong, Qiyuan Wang, Xinlan Tang, Sungjong Kim, Chan Hee Park, Byeng D. Youn, Guo Dong Goh, Xi Huang, Wai Yee Yeong, Yung C Shin, He Zhang, Zitong Wang, Fei Tao, Jagjit Singh Srai, Satyandra K. Gupta, Byung Gun Joung, Albin John, John W. Sutherland, Sang Won Lee, Olga Fink, Vinay Sharma, Faez Ahmed, Wei Chen, Mark Fuge, Arild Waaler, Martin G. Skjæveland, Dimitris Kyritsis, Wei Chen, VispiNevile Karkaria, Yi-Ping Chen, Ying-Kuan Tsai, Joseph Cohen, Xun Huan, Jing Lin, Liangwei Zhang, Gregory W. Vogl, Aaron W. Cornelius, Xiaodong Jia, Dai-Yan Ji, Takanobu Minami, Ruoxin Wang
arXiv:2605.00839v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The evolution of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) is reshaping smart manufacturing by providing new capabilities for efficiency, adaptability, and autonomy across industrial value chains. However, the deployment of AI and ML in industrial settings still faces critical challenges, including the complexity of industrial big data, effective data management, integration with heterogeneous sensing and control systems, and the demand for trustworthy, explainable, and reliable operation in high-stakes industrial environments. In this roadmap, we present a comprehensive perspective on the foundations, applications, and emerging directions of AI and ML in smart manufacturing. It is structured in three parts. The first highlights…

item.title: LUMINA: A Grid Foundation Model for Benchmarking AC Optimal Power Flow Surrogate Learning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Hongwei Jin, Keunju Song, Zeeshan Memon, Yijiang Li, Stefano Fenu, Hongseok Kim, Liang Zhao, Kibaek Kim
arXiv:2605.02133v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AC optimal power flow (ACOPF) is foundational yet computationally expensive in power grid operations, driving learning-based surrogates for large-scale grid analysis. These surrogates, however, often fail to generalize across network topologies, a critical gap for deployment on grids not seen during training and for routine operational what-if studies. We introduce LUMINA-Bench, a comprehensive benchmark suite for ACOPF surrogate learning covering multi-topology pretraining, transfer, and adaptation. The benchmark evaluates homogeneous and heterogeneous architectures under single- and multi-topology learning settings using unified metrics that capture both predictive accuracy and physics-informed constraint violations. We additionally compar…

item.title: 基于物理信息神经网络利用观测数据预测空间碎片轨道
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Gokulraj S, Antony Xavier Bronson F
该工作提出将轨道力学方程嵌入深度神经网络训练目标的物理信息神经网络（PINN）框架，以应对非线性引力摄动、大气阻力变化及太阳辐射压力等长期累积效应。系统以位置、速度、历元等观测状态为输入，输出可配置未来区间的轨迹预报。利用公开两行根数数据集结合模拟阻力摄动剖面进行验证，针对低地球轨道目标开展评估。

item.title: 基于凝聚基态的位移型铁电体自洽计算框架
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 F. Yang, L. Q. Chen
该工作发展了一种基于量子统计描述极化涨落的自洽微观计算框架，用于预测位移型铁电体的有限温度热力学性质与相变临界行为。该方法仅需基态性质即可预测全温区介电/铁电响应，无需Landau-Ginzburg理论所需的先验临界知识或温度依赖参数拟合。应用于经典铁电体PbTiO₃、量子顺电体SrTiO₃和KTaO₃，以及应变铁电SrTiO₃，与实验测量在全温区取得显著定量一致。

item.title: 弱无序铁磁体中饱和磁化强度与自旋输运的统一方程
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sumanta Mukherjee
该工作为弱无序自旋-1/2铁磁体提供饱和磁化强度损失与自旋输运的统一描述框架。考虑有限尺寸效应分布，导出统一的Bloch方程形式，并进一步扩展获得此类系统中自旋输运的统一表达式，为理解弱无序铁磁体的磁性及输运性质建立连贯的理论基础。

item.title: 笼目磁体Nd₃Ru₄Al₁₂中具有减小磁矩长度的共线铁磁性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yuki Ishihara, Ryota Nakano, Rinsuke Yamada, Takuya Nomoto, Priya R. Baral, Moritz M. Hirschmann, Kamini Gautam, Kamil K. Kolincio, Akiko Kikkawa, Seno Aji, Hiraku Saitoh, Masaaki Matsuda, Yasujiro Taguchi, Taka-hisa Arima, Yoshinori Tokura, Taro Nakajima, Max Hirschberger
通过单晶中子衍射和极化中子实验，作者确定笼目晶格磁体Nd₃Ru₄Al₁₂的磁基态为具有均匀磁矩长度m_c=2.1 μB/Nd和 Ordering矢量Q=0的共线铁磁态（"hex-FM"），修正了此前关于两个Nd位点不等磁矩长度（"ortho-FM"态）的报道。极化中子散射的翻转比分析与hex-FM态一致，为理解该材料在T_C=41 K附近大的涨落诱导霍尔和能斯特响应提供了微观基础。

item.title: 成分调控的范德华铁电体NbOI₂₋ₓClₓ合金光学与电学性质
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Gaolei Zhao, Juhe Liu, Jinkai Huo, Tian Han, Yunhao Tong, Hu Wang, Konstantin Kozadaev, Andrei Zheltkovich, Changsen Sun, Alexei Tolstik, Andrey Novitsky, Lujun Pan, Dawei Li
通过化学气相输运法可控制备了高结晶性NbOI₂₋ₓClₓ范德华合金，实验结合第一性原理计算表明其晶格、声子模式和能带结构可在NbOI₂与NbOCl₂之间连续调控。二次谐波产生信号的振幅和偏振均呈现显著的成分依赖性，为可调面内铁电特性提供了光学证据；基于该合金的场效应晶体管表现出稳健的n型半导体行为，阈值电压和载流子迁移率可被精确调控。

item.title: Computational prediction of ferromagnetic AT6X6 kagome compounds
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Shiya Chen, Zhen Zhang, Vladimir Antropov, Yang Sun
arXiv:2512.13431v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We present a systematic high-throughput density-functional-theory investigation of the structural and magnetic stability of 312 substitutional compounds in the magnetic kagome AT6X6 family. Our screening confirms the stability of many previously reported structures and predicts several additional stable candidates. Within collinear spin configurations, we find that Fe-based systems predominantly adopt antiferromagnetic ground states, whereas Mn-based analogues exhibit a more balanced distribution between ferromagnetic and antiferromagnetic order. For compounds exhibiting several nearly degenerate collinear configurations, we analyze the nature of their magnetic ground states, assess the possible emergence of non-collinear order, and disc…

item.title: Cr掺杂FeSb₂中交替磁性有序的证据
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 A K M Ashiquzzaman Shawon, Eoghan Downey, Shane Smolenski, Thomas J. Hicken, Amir Henderson, Mingyu Xu, Trisha Musall, Rafael Lopes Sabainsk, Yuan Zhu, Weiwei Xie, Elena Gati, Lu Li, Zurab Guguchia, Na Hyun Jo
在Fe₀.₈₅Cr₀.₁₅Sb₂单晶中，磁化测量显示约3.5 K以下出现沿b轴的自旋补偿有序；输运测量揭示正到负磁阻的转变，反常霍尔响应在T_N以下出现，表明时间反演对称性破缺。μ子自旋弛豫证实3.5 K以下存在体相磁有序，支持Cr掺杂FeSb₂作为无净磁化而破缺时间反演对称性的交替磁性候选材料。

item.title: 受挫蜂窝晶格反铁磁体中的奇异磁性与持续短程自旋关联
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 M. Barik, Q. Faure, F. Damay, J. P. Embs, S. Petit, P. Khuntia
该工作报道了S=5/2畸变蜂窝磁体CaZn₂Fe(PO₄)₃的晶体结构、磁化、比热和非弹性中子散射测量结果。磁化测量揭示Fe³⁺ (S=5/2)矩之间的主导反铁磁相互作用，外场下磁化率中的凹陷及其场演化表明非常规场诱导相变，零场比热显示T_N≈1.67 K的反铁磁相变并随磁场演化，暗示场诱导自旋倾斜态的稳定化。热力学测量揭示转变温度以上的短程自旋关联，非弹性中子散射进一步表征了该高自旋受挫蜂窝体系中经典与量子磁性交织导致的奇异激发行为。

item.title: 洁净磁性半导体GdGaI中的场致金属-绝缘体转变、陈绝缘体与拓扑半金属
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Kazuki Guzman, Hiroaki Ishizuka
该工作基于磁性半导体GdGaI中最近发现的四子格triple-q非共面磁有序，研究低载流子密度半导体中自旋-电荷耦合重塑电子结构并诱导非平庸拓扑相的有效理论。模型将Γ点Ga 4p空穴口袋与M点三个Gd 5d电子口袋通过四个交换通道耦合；对于净磁化为零的反铁磁伞状态，体系存在平庸（C=0）与C=±4陈绝缘体相，其间由金属区隔开。通过推导Γ点低能有效理论，证明拓扑相界由两个简并双外尔半金属描述，自然解释陈数ΔC=4的跃变。无外场调谐倾斜角可驱动陈绝缘体相的绝缘体-金属转变，详情需查阅原文。

item.title: 螺旋反铁磁体中场诱导的非公度-公度相变
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 P. T. Bolokhova, A. V. Syromyatnikov
该工作解析研究了具有易平面各向异性的海森堡反铁磁体，其中磁螺旋由Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya相互作用和/或交换耦合阻挫诱导。小面内磁场可逐渐改变磁结构波矢k₀，当k₀接近倒格矢g/n（n为整数）时，可产生非公度与公度磁有序相之间的转变。推导了n=2,3,4的临界场解析表达式，并将其应用于三角晶格化合物RbFe(MoO₄)₂，同时确定了更精确描述该材料全部实验数据的模型参数。

item.title: 无序材料和异质结构中超导性与交替磁性的相互作用
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Rodrigo de las Heras, Tim Kokkeler, Stefan Ilić, Ilya V. Tokatly, F. Sebastian Bergeret
该工作基于量子动力学输运方程研究无序系统中超导性与交替磁性的耦合，导出Ginzburg-Landau自由能并发现自旋与超导序参量空间变化的耦合项。由此产生两种效应：超流诱导自旋纹理的非线性磁电效应，以及序参量幅度空间变化导致的磁化；二者在Abrikosov涡旋中竞争。幅度变化效应还在超导体-交替磁体异质结构中产生邻近诱导磁化。

item.title: 自旋电子学与磁子学讲义
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 M. Mazanov, V. A. Shklovskij
该系列讲义系统阐述磁子学与自旋电子学的核心理论概念。内容涵盖量子力学、连续介质电动力学及磁学基础，继而讨论磁动力学经典理论（铁磁/反铁磁共振、动态磁化率、自旋波），重点分析自旋流、交换自旋流、自旋力矩、自旋霍尔效应及相关磁电阻现象。特别详述自旋转移力矩与自旋泵浦效应的Landauer量子多通道散射矩阵推导，并对比反铁磁与铁磁动力学的关键差异，指出反铁磁材料在自旋电子学与磁子学中的独特应用前景。

item.title: 交错磁体中的轨道劈裂流
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Koushik Ghorai, Sayan Sarkar, Amit Agarwal
该工作提出轨道劈裂流（OSC）概念，作为自旋劈裂流的轨道类比，并在交错磁体中利用密度矩阵框架推导其Drude贡献与轨道贝里曲率贡献。d波交错磁体FeSb₂因镜面对称性强制轨道磁矩为零而完全抑制Drude通道，实现纯内禀OSC。OSC响应具有强各向异性，在特定场方向下超过自旋劈裂流近四倍。该工作进一步证明OSC可在交错磁体-铁磁体异质结中产生阻尼类力矩，与自旋劈裂流协同显著降低磁化翻转时间。

item.title: AgI (110)表面的持久自旋纹理与自旋-轨道霍尔响应
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Manish Kumar Mohanta
该工作结合第一性原理计算与解析模型，系统研究AgI (110)表面的结构、电子和自旋-轨道输运性质。系统的非中心对称和非简单对称性产生稳健的持久自旋纹理（PST），特征为单向自旋构型和抑制的自旋弛豫，实现有效无限自旋寿命。与此前主要基于硫族化合物的PST材料不同，该工作证明卤化物半导体亦可承载PST，显著拓展自旋电子学应用的材料平台。有效自旋-轨道耦合哈密顿量再现能带中的各向异性自旋劈裂和动量偏移；工作还提出两个新的PST解析模型并与现有模型比较，同时系统表现出可观的本征自旋霍尔电导率和轨道霍尔响应。

item.title: 铌掺杂调控范德华铁磁体CrTe₂的自旋动力学
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Dhan Raj Lawati, Prem Bahadur Karki, Jitender Kumar, Karishma Prasad, Mohamed A. Elekhtiar, Kai Huang, Bibek Tiwari, Suvechhya Lamichhane, Rupak Timalsina, Zane Hubble, Ayodimeji E. Aregbesola, John Watt, Sy-Hwang Liou, Evgeny Y. Tsymbal, Jian Wang, Kapildeb Ambal, Abdelghani Laraoui
该工作通过Nb替代Cr₁₋ₓNbₓTe₂（x=0–0.2）系统调控二维范德华铁磁体1T-CrTe₂的自旋动力学。铁磁共振谱学显示Nb掺杂可将共振频率从40 GHz宽范围调至几GHz，Gilbert阻尼常数从~0.066适度增至~0.14，同时保持室温铁磁性。磁测量显示居里温度和饱和磁化强度随Nb含量降低，DFT计算归因于磁各向异性和交换相互作用的Nb诱导修饰。~80 nm薄片比块体具有更低共振频率和阻尼。

item.title: 交错磁体中的自旋-轨道耦合效应：弱自旋与轨道铁磁性及电子态相对论劈裂的 interplay
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 L. M. Sandratskii, K. Carva, V. M. Silkin
该工作深入探讨交错磁体中相对论效应的形成机制，聚焦弱铁磁性（WFM）现象，结合相对论密度泛函计算与基于自旋空间群的对称性分析。在原子尺度，计算了弱铁磁态的自旋与轨道原子磁矩，发现WFM矩随自旋-轨道耦合强度呈非单调依赖；准对称计算（仅保留沿Néel矢量的SOC分量）揭示自旋WFM消失而轨道WFM存在，表明自旋与轨道磁矩的形成存在本质差异。在电子态层面，引入电子态磁结构（MSES）概念，研究个体电子态的性质，详情需查阅原文。

item.title: 非均匀交错磁性中间层诱导的自旋极化约瑟夫森电流
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Wenjun Zhao, Yuri Fukaya, Pablo Burset, Jorge Cayao, Yukio Tanaka, Bo Lu
该工作提出一种利用具有面内奈尔矢量的非均匀交错磁性中间层构建的无外场约瑟夫森结。研究表明，约瑟夫森电流的相位关系和临界电流对两层奈尔矢量间的失配角高度敏感：当失配角为π且层厚相等时，超导配对振幅的空间振荡相互抵消，显著增强临界电流并消除0-π跃迁。奈尔矢量的非共线排列进一步产生净自旋极化约瑟夫森电流，作为自旋三重态配对关联的明确特征，为无能耗自旋电子器件提供高度可调的无场平台。

item.title: 矢量磁子学：交错磁体中自旋流体的电注入与调控
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yanmeng Lei, Rui-Chun Xiao, Weiwei Lin, Tao Yu
作者预测自旋积累可向交错磁体电注入包含纵向和可观横向分量的"矢量"或多方向磁子自旋流，该横向电流在远离源区时发生符号反转，且可通过重新取向奈尔矢开关。量子动力学计算表明，由于宇称-时间对称性破缺，交错磁体中的横向响应较常规反铁磁体增强两个数量级，为实验区分交错磁体与常规反铁磁体提供了决定性的输运指纹。

item.title: 强自旋-轨道与交换相互作用Ni/Bi双层超导体中的涡旋输运
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Laxmipriya Nanda, Sohini Guin, Yasen Hou, Rajib Sarkar, Naresh Shyaga, Souvik Banerjee, A. Sundaresan, N. S. Vidhyadhiraja, Jagadeesh S. Moodera, Dhavala Suri
该工作以Ni/Bi双层为研究平台，探索强自旋轨道耦合磁性环境中的Cooper配对。铁磁Ni与仅在约10 mK以下超导的强自旋轨道金属Bi耦合形成的双层在3-4 K表现出超导性。近Tc区的磁输运研究揭示了涡旋动力学和交换近邻效应：孤立涡旋对垂直外场敏感响应，产生可归因于Magnus力与粘滞力竞争的反对称横向电阻峰。以铁磁绝缘体为对照实验确认超导性贯穿整个双层而非仅局限于界面，结果表明观测可由常规s波序参量统一解释，非常规贡献较为细微。

item.title: 谷光子晶体波导中的谷锁定光学自旋斯格明子
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Lvjin He, Shanshan Chen, Ziyang Chen, Lan Zhang, Lipeng Wan, Weimin Deng, Tianbao Yu
光学斯格明子因其在超精密计量、光学信息处理和量子技术中的应用前景而受到广泛关注。本研究展示了一种光子平台，利用拓扑保护的谷边缘态实现光学自旋斯格明子的片上鲁棒定向传输。这些斯格明子源于谷光子晶体表面倏逝场中的自旋-轨道耦合，作为拓扑保护边缘态的本征态存在，确保其鲁棒单向传播。利用拓扑边缘态的谷自由度，研究者进一步实现了谷锁定的自旋斯格明子，实现了对自旋斯格明子极性的灵活调控。

item.title: Enhanced Magnetoelectric coupling in Praseodymium doped multiferroïc bismuth ferrites
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 A. AMOURI, D. Dhruv, W. DIMASSI
The polycrystalline ceramic synthesis was obtained through a solid-state reaction under a controlled atmosphere. The X-ray diffraction Rietveld refinement showed a rhombohedral R3c structure for all our ceramics. Room temperature M vs. H and P vs. E hysteresis loops revealed improved magnetic and ferroelectric properties due to Praseodymium substitution. The ceramic presents attainable saturated hysteresis loops with a spontaneous polarization (Ps) of 18.05 µC/cm2. Pr substitution enhances magnetization, reaching the uppermost remnant magnetization (Mr) of 6.73 emu/g for x = 0.025 composition. Moreover, Both Tc and TN shifted closer to room temperature and approached each other for ceramics, indicating enhanced magnetoelectric coupling. At room temperature, the highest value of αME was ob…

item.title: 交错磁邻近效应
item.description:
📚 Editors' Suggestions
👤 Ziye Zhu, Richang Huang, Xianzhang Chen, Zhou Cui, Xunkai Duan, Jiayong Zhang, Igor Žutić, and Tong Zhou
该工作理论预言并研究了交错磁邻近效应：与非磁性材料相邻的交错磁体可使其获得交错磁性。这一效应为在非磁性材料中诱导产生交错磁性提供了新途径，详情需查阅原文。

item.title: Influence of A-site Mg2+ Incorporation on the Structural, Optical, Dielectric, and Ferroelectric Properties of BaZr0.1Ti 0.9O3 Perovskite Ceramics
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Anitha G, Pavithra G, Geetha D, Giridharan N. V
Lead-free ferroelectric ceramics have emerged as critical alternatives to toxic lead-based systems for next-generation dielectric and energy storage applications. In this work, Ba1−yMgyZr0.1Ti0.9O3 (y = 0.00, 0.05, 0.07, 0.10) ceramics were synthesised via citrate-nitrate auto-combustion and systematically investigated to understand the influence of A-site Mg2+ substitution on structural, optical, dielectric, ferroelectric, and energy-storage properties. Rietveld refinement confirmed phase-pure tetragonal symmetry (P4mm) across all compositions, with progressive lattice contraction from 4.0266 Å to 3.9909 Å, while average crystallite size decreased from 21.71 nm (BZT) to 17.21 nm (BMZT0.10) as Mg content increased. HR-SEM revealed a monotonically decreasing grain size from 116.87 nm to 11…

item.title: 层状反铁磁半金属EuSn₂As₂的本征负磁阻
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 K. S. Pervakov, A. V. Sadakov, O. A. Sobolevskiy, V. A. Vlasenko, V. P. Martovitsky, E. A. Sedov, E. I. Maltsev, P. D. Grigoriev, N. S. Pavlov, I. A. Nekrasov, O. E. Tereshchenko, V. A. Golyashov, and V. M. Pudalov
该工作综合磁输运测量、ARPES能带结构、DFT计算和解析理论，揭示高度各向异性半金属EuSn₂As₂中各向同性负磁阻的成因。通过系统研究阐明层状反铁磁半金属中本征负磁阻的物理机制。

item.title: Accelerating Noisy Variational Quantum Algorithms with Physics-Informed Denoising Networks
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jie Liu, Xin Wang
arXiv:2605.02066v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Variational quantum algorithms are promising for near-term quantum computing, but are severely limited by hardware noise and the substantial circuit overhead required for error mitigation methods such as Zero-Noise Extrapolation (ZNE). We propose a Physics-Informed Denoising Network (PIDN) that reduces the cost of ZNE by learning a surrogate model of its optimization dynamics. By viewing the variational update as a trajectory in the parameter space, PIDN is trained to reproduce ZNE-mitigated expectation values and gradient directions while incorporating a physics-informed loss that preserves the gradient descent dynamics. Once trained, PIDN replaces repeated multi-noise evaluations with denoised expectation and gradient estimation directly…

item.title: Learning symmetry-protected topological order from trapped-ion experiments
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Nicolas Sadoune, Ivan Pogorelov, Claire L. Edmunds, Giuliano Giudici, Giacomo Giudice, Christian D. Marciniak, Martin Ringbauer, Thomas Monz, Lode Pollet
arXiv:2408.05017v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Classical machine learning has proven remarkably useful in post-processing quantum data, yet typical learning algorithms often require prior training to be effective. In this work, we employ a tensorial kernel support vector machine (TK-SVM) to analyze experimental data produced by trapped-ion quantum computers. This unsupervised method benefits from directly interpretable training parameters, allowing it to identify the non-trivial string-order characterizing symmetry-protected topological (SPT) phases. We apply our technique to two examples: a spin-1/2 model and a spin-1 model, featuring the cluster state and the AKLT state as paradigmatic instances of SPT order, respectively. Using matrix product states, we generate a family of…

item.title: Shortcut learning in geometric knot classification
item.description:
📚 Machine Learning: Science and Technology
👤 Djordje Mihajlovic and Davide Michieletto
Classifying the topology of closed curves is a central problem in low dimensional topology with applications beyond mathematics spanning protein folding, polymer physics and even magnetohydrodynamics. The central problem is how to determine whether two embeddings of a closed arc are equivalent under ambient isotopy. Given the striking ability of neural networks to solve complex classification tasks, it is therefore natural to ask if the knot classification problem can be tackled using machine learning (ML). In this paper, we investigate generic shortcut methods employed by ML to solve the knot classification challenge and specifically discover hidden non-topological features in training data generated through molecular dynamics simulations of polygonal knots that are used by ML to arrive…

item.title: Physics-Constrained Learning of Dose-Dependent Spectral Degradation in Metal--Organic Frameworks from In Situ Low-Loss EELS
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Gabriel T. dos Santos, Roberto dos Reis, Vinayak P. Dravid
arXiv:2605.01697v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Electron-beam irradiation limits atomic-resolution characterization of beam-sensitive hybrid materials, yet quantitative models that connect in situ spectroscopy to dose-dependent degradation remain scarce. Here we use a physics-informed neural network (PINN) to model beam-induced spectral evolution in MIL-101(Fe) from an in situ low-loss electron energy-loss spectroscopy (EELS) dose series. Each spectrum is reduced to fixed-window low-loss descriptors, tilde nₑff,ⱼ(Φ)=intW_ⱼS(E,Φ),dE, evaluated over nominal π--π*, C--C, C--O, and M--O windows. These descriptors are relative window-integrated low-loss spectral areas, not absolute f-sum-rule effective electron numbers. For each spectral channel, a latent integrity variable Cᵢ₍Φ) obeys the sam…

item.title: EuLearn: a 3D database for learning Euler characteristics
item.description:
📚 Machine Learning: Science and Technology
👤 Pablo Suárez-Serrato, Rodrigo Fritz, Victor Mijangos, Anayanzi Martínez and Eduardo Velazquez Richards
We present EuLearn6, the first surface datasets equitably representing a diversity of topological types. We designed our embedded surfaces of uniformly varying genera relying on random knots, thus allowing our surfaces to knot with themselves. EuLearn contributes new topological datasets of meshes, point clouds, and scalar fields in 3D. We aim to facilitate the training of machine learning systems that can discern topological features. We experimented with specific emblematic 3D neural network architectures, finding that their vanilla implementations perform poorly on genus classification. To enhance performance, we developed a novel, non-Euclidean, statistical sampling method adapted to graph and manifold data. We also introduce adjacency-informed adaptations of PointNet and Transformer…

item.title: Intrinsic Neuromorphic Behaviors in PEDOT/PSS Nanoscale Networks and their Enhancement via Ethylene Glycol Doping
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Takuya Matsumoto, SURAJIT SARKAR, Yuta Nishimura, Hitomi Hokonohara, Masahiro Nakayama, Yuma Kondo 等8位作者
As artificial intelligence expands across all aspects of society, the demand for computational resources and electrical power continues to grow, motivating the exploration of energy-efficient alternatives to conventional computing. Physical computation, which exploits the intrinsic properties of materials, has therefore attracted increasing attention. Here, we demonstrate that solid-state PEDOT/PSS network films connected to nanoscale electrodes exhibit nonlinear and history-dependent current responses, enabling intrinsic neuromorphic behavior. Benchmark waveform generation tasks show significantly higher accuracy than microscale devices, indicating that PEDOT/PSS nanoscale networks function as intrinsic neural networks. Incorporation of ethylene glycol (EG) further enhances neuromorphic…

item.title: Chebyshev-Augmented One-Shot Transfer Learning for PINNs on Nonlinear Differential Equations
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yiqi Rao, Pavlos Protopapas
arXiv:2605.01634v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) offer a flexible paradigm for solving differential equations by embedding governing laws into the training objective. A persistent limitation is instance specificity: standard PINNs typically require retraining for each new forcing term, boundary/initial condition, or parameter setting. One-shot transfer learning (OTL) addresses this bottleneck for linear operators by freezing a pretrained latent representation and computing optimal output weights in closed form, but for nonlinear problems closed-form adaptation is generally unavailable because the loss is nonconvex in the output layer. In this paper we substantially broaden the class of nonlinearities amenable to one-shot PINN transfer by combining O…

item.title: Physics-Informed Neural Learning for State Reconstruction and Parameter Identification in Coupled Greenhouse Climate Dynamics
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sani Biswas, Khursheed J. Ansari, Md. Nasim Akhtar
arXiv:2605.02524v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) have recently emerged as a promising framework for integrating data-driven learning with physical knowledge. In this work, we propose a coupled PINN approach for the joint reconstruction of indoor temperature and humidity dynamics in greenhouse environments, together with simultaneous identification of key model parameters. The method incorporates a reduced-order physically motivated model into the learning process, enabling consistent estimation under sparse and noisy observations. The artificial intelligence contribution lies in the development of a coupled physics-informed neural learning framework that integrates governing dynamical constraints into neural network training, while the engineering a…

item.title: Variational Matrix-Learning Fourier Networks for Parametric Multiphysics Surrogates
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Xinyu Li, Jianhua Zhang, Liang Chen
arXiv:2605.02280v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multiphysics simulation is critical for system-technology co-optimization (STCO) in chiplet-based design, but repeated finite-element solutions of PDE-governed problems are computationally expensive in parametric design exploration. This paper proposes a variational matrix-learning Fourier network (VMLFN) for efficient parametric multiphysics surrogate modeling. VMLFN constructs a log-space sine neural representation with randomly sampled spectral frequencies, frequency-dependent decay regulation, and embedded Dirichlet boundary conditions. With fixed hidden-layer parameters, the output-layer weights are determined by reformulating the governing PDEs into variational weak forms and enforcing the stationarity condition of the resulting energy…

item.title: Topological Neural Tangent Kernel
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sanjukta Krishnagopal
arXiv:2605.01110v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Graph neural tangent kernels give a principled infinite-width theory for graph neural networks, but inherit a basic limitation of graph models: they see only pairwise structure. Many relational systems contain higher-order interactions that are more naturally represented by simplicial complexes. We introduce the Topological Neural Tangent Kernel (TopoNTK), an infinite-width kernel for simplicial message passing on edge features. TopoNTK combines lower Hodge interactions, capturing graph-like coupling through shared vertices, with upper Hodge interactions, capturing coupling through filled simplices. This makes the kernel sensitive to topology invisible to graph kernels, allowing complexes with the same graph but different filled simplices to…

item.title: GraphSculptor: Sculpting Pre-training Coreset for Graph Self-supervised Learning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Chuang Liu, Zelin Yao, Xueqi Ma, Luzhi Wang, Mukun Chen, Pinghua Xu, Wenbin Hu
arXiv:2605.01310v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Graph self-supervised learning typically relies on large-scale unlabeled datasets, heavily inflating computational costs. However, empirical evidence suggests that these datasets contain substantial redundancy-our analysis reveals that uniformly subsampling 50% of graphs retains over 96% of downstream performance. To exploit this redundancy, we introduce GraphSculptor for pre-training coreset construction. Unlike methods dependent on additional training-time signals or limited solely to topological statistics, GraphSculptor provides a label-free solution that constructs coresets via two complementary perspectives: intrinsic structure and contextual semantics. Concretely, structural diversity is quantified using intrinsic graph statistics, yi…

item.title: Rhamba: Region-Aware Hybrid Attention-Mamba Framework for Self-Supervised Learning in Resting-State fMRI
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ruthwik Reddy Doodipala, Pankaj Pandey, Pratheek Eranki, Carolina Torres-Rojas, Manob Jyoti Saikia, Ranganatha Sitaram
arXiv:2605.01240v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Self-supervised pretraining is promising for large-scale neuroimaging, yet the impact of region-aware masking and hybrid sequence modeling remains underexplored. In this work, we introduce Rhamba, a region-aware pretraining framework that integrates anatomically guided masking with hybrid Attention-Mamba architectures for resting state functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) analysis. Models were pretrained on the ABIDE dataset using region-aligned patch embeddings and three masking strategies (Any, Majority, and Pure) with increasing spatial specificity. We evaluated four architectural variants: a Mamba only model, an Alternate architecture with interleaved Mamba and Attention blocks, and two hybrid encoder-decoder configurations (Atte…

item.title: Ultrafast On-chip Online Learning via Spline Locality in Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Duc Hoang, Aarush Gupta, Philip Harris
arXiv:2602.02056v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Ultrafast online learning is essential for high-frequency systems, such as controls for quantum computing and nuclear fusion, where adaptation must occur on sub-microsecond timescales. Meeting these requirements demands low-latency, fixed-precision computation under strict memory constraints, a regime in which conventional Multi-Layer Perceptrons (MLPs) are both inefficient and numerically unstable. We identify key properties of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) that align with these constraints. Specifically, we show that: (i) KAN updates exploiting B-spline locality are sparse, enabling superior on-chip resource scaling, and (ii) KANs are inherently robust to fixed-point quantization. By implementing fixed-point online training o…

item.title: Learning Stratigraphically Consistent Relative Geologic Time from 3D Seismic Data via Sinusoidal Mapping
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yimin Dou, Xinming Wu, Hui Gao, Zhengfa Bi
arXiv:2605.01273v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Relative Geologic Time (RGT) estimation from seismic data is a cornerstone of subsurface structural modeling, depositional evolution analysis, and reservoir characterization, supporting horizon correlation and depositional system reconstruction. Yet accurate RGT estimation remains challenging: RGT is intrinsically a topologically constrained continuous field, in which local errors readily propagate globally and distort the overall result. Conventional methods rely heavily on priors, attribute extraction, and manual interaction, leading to cumbersome workflows. Existing deep-learning approaches mostly use a regression formulation with pixel-wise MSE/MAE losses, which struggle to capture thin horizons and fail to model the stratigraphic semant…

item.title: Persistent Homology of Time Series through Complex Networks
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 İsmail Güzel
arXiv:2605.01624v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We present a unified pipeline for univariate time series classification via complex networks and persistent homology. A time series is mapped to a graph through one of five constructions across three families (visibility (natural and horizontal visibility graphs), transition, and proximity) and the graph is converted to a dissimilarity matrix from which a Vietoris-Rips filtration yields persistence diagrams. These diagrams are vectorized into fixed-length features through persistence landscapes and topological summary statistics. By standardizing the downstream processing, differences in classification performance are attributable to the network construction and distance metric alone. Experiments on twelve UCR benchmarks show that (i) no s…

item.title: Hybrid Quantum Reinforcement Learning with QAOA for Improved Vehicle Routing Optimization
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 T. Satyanarayana Murthy, B. Swathi Sowmya, Santhosh Voruganti, Sai Varshini Giridi, Chaitanyya Pratap Agarwal, Vanteddu Akshitha
arXiv:2605.01574v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vehicle Routing Problem (VRP) is one of the most complex NP-hard combinatorial optimization problem in transportation and logistics that requires a dynamic solution approach. In this paper we present a new hybrid approach that combines the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) into the QRL policy network, instead of the usual variational layers, QAOA mixing and cost Hamiltonian layers. This enhancement enables the agent to exploit problem specific particular quantum correlations when learning policies, and so richer exploration of the routing solution space. The QAOA-augmented QRL framework shows quicker convergence in training and can tackle larger VRP instances that are beyond the reach of Grover's Adaptive Search (GAS) and Qua…

item.title: Machine Learning Enhanced Laser Spectroscopy for Multi-Species Gas Detection in Complex and Harsh Environments
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Mohamed Sy
arXiv:2605.01306v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Laser absorption spectroscopy (LAS) is a well-established technique for non-intrusive measurement of gas species in combustion and atmospheric environments, but conventional methods struggle with multi-species mixtures under dynamic or interference-laden conditions. Overlapping spectral features, noise, and incomplete reference data limit reliability when unknown or weakly absorbing species are present. This dissertation develops diagnostics combining LAS with machine learning (ML) to address these limitations. Deep denoising autoencoders (DDAEs) are applied to shock-tube measurements during high-speed hydrocarbon pyrolysis, improving signal fidelity and detection limits for trace species. A structured unsupervised framework, HT-SIMNet, then…

item.title: 基于强化学习的观测器控制用于纯方位跟踪
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Branko Ristic, Sanjeev Arulampalam
该研究将纯方位跟踪的观测器机动问题表述为信念马尔可夫决策过程，以容积卡尔曼滤波（CKF）后验表示信念状态，设计兼顾目标位置估计误差（欧氏距离）与CKF估计一致性（马氏距离）的奖励函数，以权重因子β∈[0,1]在帕累托前沿进行几何插值。策略通过50,000回合训练的深度Q网络（DQN）实现，5,000次蒙特卡洛评估表明β=0.7的DQN策略在精度与鲁棒性间取得最佳平衡，匹配信息论基线的平均跟踪精度同时降低最坏情况误差。

item.title: Spectral Model eXplainer: a chemically-grounded explainability framework for spectral-based machine learning models
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jose Vinicius Ribeiro, Rafael Figueira Goncalves, Fabio Luiz Melquiades, Sylvio Barbon Junior
arXiv:2605.02684v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Spectral-based machine learning models have been increasingly deployed in chemometrics and spectroscopy, where predictive accuracy is as important as explainability. Current employed eXplainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) methods are largely adapted from tabular or generic multivariate domains, assigning relevance to isolated spectral variables rather than to the chemically meaningful spectral zones. Widely adopted tools such as SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP), Permutation Feature Importance (PFI), and Variable Importance in Projection scores (VIP) were not designed for the physical continuity and high collinearity of spectral data, and their variable-level outputs require post-hoc aggregation to recover zone-level information. Thi…

item.title: 机器学习增强NOMA结合AI驱动功率分配用于多RIS辅助6G太赫兹网络
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Mohanad Mezher, Mustafa Mohammed Abdulkareem
太赫兹通信是6G无线网络物理层的关键技术，可提供10 GHz/信道的超高带宽和超过1 Tbps的数据率，但面临距离相关路径损耗、分子吸收和信号遮挡等挑战。可重构智能表面(RIS)通过可编程无源波束赋形调控信号传播，非正交多址(NOMA)则通过功率域叠加编码和连续干扰消除提升频谱效率。该研究提出机器学习增强的NOMA框架，利用离线训练的有监督神经网络将级联信道状态信息映射为优化功率分配策略，以提升多RIS辅助太赫兹网络性能。

item.title: An ALE-Consistent Graph Neural Operator-Transformer Framework for Fluid-Structure Interaction
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Shihang Zhao, Martín Saravia, Haokui Jiang, Zhiyang Xue, Shunxiang Cao
arXiv:2605.00937v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose an arbitrary Lagrangian-Eulerian (ALE)-consistent machine learning framework for long-term fluid-structure interaction (FSI) prediction on deforming unstructured meshes. Specifically, the fluid dynamics are modeled by a surrogate that combines a graph neural operator (GNO) with a vision Transformer (ViT) for spatiotemporal prediction, while a lightweight long short-term memory (LSTM) network predicts structural kinematics at the interface. The two surrogates are coupled through a standard partitioned procedure. Most importantly, kinematic compatibility at the moving interface is enforced via an ALE-consistent boundary-correction step that updates the fluid-side interface velocity with the predicted structural velocity at each coup…

item.title: Autonomous Reliability Qualification of Ga₂O₃₋based Hydrogen and Temperature Sensors via Safe Active Learning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Davi Febba, William A. Callahan, Anna Sacchi, Andriy Zakutayev
arXiv:2605.00868v2 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We present a Safe Active Learning (SAL) framework for autonomous reliability characterization of rectifying Ga₂O₃₋based devices under coupled thermal and hydrogen stress. SAL treats rectification as a device-physics-motivated safety observable and models its evolution over elapsed time, temperature, and H₂ concentration using a Gaussian-process surrogate. To handle condition-dependent and uncertain experiment durations, the method combines an adaptive completion-time window, time-window lower-confidence-bound safety checks, a trust region anchored to previously verified safe conditions, and a two-phase strategy that transitions from conservative safe exploration to progressively relaxed rectification targets as the device degrades. We firs…

item.title: 深度神经网络的几何与谱对齐 I
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ziran Liu, Wei Wang, Jinhao Wang, Pengcheng Wang, Xinyi Sui, Cihan Ruan, Nam Ling, Wei Jiang
深度残差架构被建模为近恒等雅可比矩阵的乘积。本文证明了Frobenius归一化层因子的奇异谱的确定性商几何估计，强调归一化顶部径向Cartan坐标与拟合幂律图表。满秩因子通过A↦A^⊤A从GL(d)映射到正锥，再映射到有序特征值数据。在Frobenius归一化下，精确幂律谱形成迹归一化Cartan轨道，该轨道是秩上的Gibbs族、Fisher信息线和Bures-Wasserstein曲线。主要刚性定理为松弛感知边界不等式：界面径向幅度、非回溯松弛和带符号残差变化控制拟合Cartan坐标的位移。在精确图表零松弛情形下，深度L预算给出指数为(log M)/L阶的漂移；一般情况下松弛和残差增量增强该界。本文还分离了标量顶部径向与完整Cartan谱控制，后者需要Bures/Hellinger残差变化。

item.title: LLM幽灵猎手：通过自适应遗忘手术式抑制幻觉
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Joseph Spracklen, Pedram Aghazadeh, Farinaz Koushanfar, Murtuza Jadliwala
该研究提出自适应遗忘（AU）框架，用于部署后精准抑制大语言模型幻觉同时保持通用能力。AU引入混合token级目标，同步强化有效输出并抑制幻觉输出，无需预指定遗忘集即可应对无限幻觉空间。针对代码生成中模型虚构不存在的软件包（导致供应链攻击风险，即"slopsquatting"攻击）问题，AU通过轻量级持续学习机制实现手术式干预，避免全量重训练的高成本与现有方法导致的严重效用退化。

item.title: 基于机器学习势与蒙特卡洛采样的缺陷高熵合金基态结构搜索
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作围绕缺陷高熵合金的基态结构搜索展开，采用机器学习势与蒙特卡洛采样相结合的计算方法。研究旨在探索缺陷构型对高熵合金稳定性的影响，具体细节需查阅原文。

item.title: Accurate, full-dimensional computations of thousands of complex vibrational eigenstates with tree tensor network states
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Henrik R. Larsson, Brieuc Le Dé, Gino E. Gamboni
arXiv:2605.00998v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tree tensor network states (TTNSs) combined with the density matrix renormalization group (DMRG) are emerging as powerful tools for vibrational and vibronic structure simulations in molecules with strong coupling and fluxionality. In this Perspective, we discuss how TTNS methods enable accurate, full-dimensional computations of thousands of eigenstates for molecular systems ranging from quartic-force-field benchmarks to molecules with strong vibronic coupling and protonated water clusters as large as the 33-dimensional Eigen ion, H₃O⁺ḑot(H₂O)₃. We emphasize the close connection and interoperability between DMRG-based TTNS methods and the multilayer multiconfiguration time-dependent Hartree method (ML-MCTDH), which share the same underlying a…

item.title: 真实生产网络中的类霍尔横向应力与沙堆临界性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Diego Vallarino
本文发展了一种结合类霍尔横向应力机制与真实生产网络基底上沙堆阈值动态的经济不稳定霍尔-沙堆模型。该模型将经济冲击类比为外场，作用于流动密集、低冗余、低容量节点，通过乘性转换函数产生系统应力。累积应力驱动离散崩塌规则和雪崩动态，其有效激活阈值随横向暴露度下降。模型基于2000-2014年世界投入产出数据库生产网络进行校准，并在2014年基底（2,283个国家-部门节点）上模拟，通过三种替代传播归一化避免行随机算子导致的机械近临界性。受控蒙特卡洛实验识别出四种有序态：稳定吸收、潜在脆弱、临界转变和雪崩态。

item.title: Quantum-inspired Techniques in Tensor Networks for Industrial Contexts
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Alejandro Mata Ali, Iñigo Perez Delgado, Aitor Moreno Fdez. de Leceta
arXiv:2404.11277v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: In this paper we present a study of the applicability and feasibility of quantum-inspired algorithms and techniques in tensor networks for industrial environments and contexts, with a compilation of the available literature and an analysis of the use cases that may be affected by such methods. In addition, we explore the limitations of such techniques in order to determine their potential scalability.

item.title: Understanding the lifetime of water with dynamic network analysis: the case of CsOH.H2O
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Graeme J. Ackland, Ciprian G. Pruteanu, John S. Loveday, Keishiro Yamashita
arXiv:2605.01045v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We describe the atomic-level motions in caesium hydroxide monohydrate (CsOHḑotH₂O), which is a chemical compound containing layers of water and hydroxide ions. At this composition, each oxygen is involved in three hydrogen bonds which, in the hexagonal structure, form a quasi-2D honeycomb lattice. While oxygen and caesium atoms form a typical crystal lattice, the dynamics of the hydrogen atoms are more complex. Here we show that the covalent and hydrogen bonds are continually interconverting, meaning that the water and hydroxyl are interconverting by proton exchange. The order-disorder transition of the water and hydroxyl proceeds by chemical reaction rather than rotation or diffusion of the molecules. A hydrogen can rotate out of the layer,…
